ac_verdicts.ac_text: createWorktreeSetupNode accepts optional { gitRunner, repoRoot } parameters for testability (injectable GitRunner pattern matches create-worktree.ts)
createWorktreeSetupNode runs git worktree add -b elab/{storyId} {worktreePath} where worktreePath = path.join(worktreeBaseDir, storyId)

On git worktree add success, worktreeRegister is called with { storyId, worktreePath, branchName }. On failure (non-zero exit code or exception), worktreeRegister is NOT called and worktreeSetup is false. Registration failure is non-blocking (returns null → logger.warn only, setup still succeeds).

createWorktreeTeardownNode accepts optional { gitRunner, repoRoot } parameters. When worktreeSetup is false or worktreePath is null, teardown returns immediately with worktreeTornDown: true (no git or KB calls).

createWorktreeTeardownNode: (1) calls worktreeGetByStory to look up KB record, (2) runs git worktree remove --force {worktreePath}, (3) calls worktreeMarkComplete with { worktreeId, status: 'abandoned' }. All errors are warn-only — never changes workflowSuccess. worktreeTornDown is always true.

Existing elab-story.test.ts tests continue to pass after the implementation change (no regressions). createElabStoryGraph compiles, runElabStory runs end-to-end, teardown always executes (AC-3 invariant preserved).
